response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad web and GIS exposure, but the role demands deep C++ or Rust expertise in routing/navigation pipelines — areas where his background is thin. His C++ exposure is limited to early-career work at National Instruments and a university thesis project, and Rust does not appear in his skill set at all. His GISCollective project shows geospatial interest but focuses on data collection for conservation rather than high-throughput routing tile generation or real-time traffic integration. The overwhelming majority of his commercial experience is in TypeScript, Node.js, and web infrastructure, which does not transfer to the systems-level, performance-critical work this role requires.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through the GISCollective open-source geo-data platform and Green Map GIS application
Low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic, including async I/O, caching, and high-throughput micro-services
Long track record of shipping production systems with quality practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js; C++ usage is limited to two early-career engagements and no Rust experience is evident
No experience with routing tile generation, navigation guidance pipelines, or real-time traffic data integration
GIS work centers on data collection and map visualization for NGOs, not the high-scale, latency-sensitive routing infrastructure the role demands
